title: KingFisher Flex Magnetic Particle Processing Plate Prep
author: Opentrons
description: This protocol automates preparation of plates that will be used in the Magnetic Particle Processing protocol on the KingFisher Flex. This protocol is still a work in progress and will be updated. Explanation of complex parameters below: * **Number of Samples**: Specify the number of samples to be run (1-96). * **Pipette Mount**: Select which mount the [P300 Single-Channel Pipette (GEN2)](https://shop.opentrons.com/collections/ot-2-pipettes/products/single-channel-electronic-pipette) is attached to. ---
steps_raw: This protocol automates preparation of plates that will be used in the Magnetic Particle Processing protocol on the KingFisher Flex. This protocol is still a work in progress and will be updated. Explanation of complex parameters below: * **Number of Samples**: Specify the number of samples to be run (1-96). * **Pipette Mount**: Select which mount the [P300 Single-Channel Pipette (GEN2)](https://shop.opentrons.com/collections/ot-2-pipettes/products/single-channel-electronic-pipette) is attached to. ---

The pipette (P300 Single-Channel Pipette (GEN2)) will pick up a new tip, transfer 500µL (175/175/150) of Wash Buffer 1 to user-specified number of wells in the KingFisher 96-Deepwell Plate in slot 4, then drop tip in the trash.
The pipette  will pick up a new tip, transfer 1000µL (200 x5) of Wash Buffer 2 to user-specified number of wells in the KingFisher 96-Deepwell Plate in slot 1, then drop tip in the trash.

The pipette  will pick up a new tip, aspirate 200µL of Elution Buffer and dispense 50µL to user-specified number of wells (aspirating 200µL more whenever needed) in the KingFisher 96-Deepwell Plate in slot 2, return any remaining Elution Buffer in the tip to the source, and finally drop the tip in the trash.

The pipette  will pick up a new tip, aspirate 140µL of Proteinase K and dispense 5µL to user-specified number of wells (in groups of 24) in the KingFisher 96-Well Microplate in slot 3, then drop tip in the trash.
materials_raw: Reservoirs for Wash Buffers (like the NEST 1-Well Reservoir, 195mL)
Reservoir for Elution Buffer (like the NEST 12-Well Reservoir, 15mL)
